followupAnswerBank.question: How do you investigate wrong data from a CDS view in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Wrong data investigation steps: 1) Reproduce — exact same selection criteria use karo jo wrong data de raha hai. 2) Compare — CDS view result vs direct table SELECT compare karo. 3) Recent transports check karo SE09 mein — koi CDS view change tha? 4) ST05 trace se actual SQL dekho — expected logic match karta hai? 5) DCL check karo — koi unauthorized rows filter ho rahi hain jo business expects? 6) Data itself check karo — source tables mein correct data hai ya data issue hai?
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Wrong data investigation: First, reproduce the exact scenario the user reported — same input, same user, same selection. Then compare the CDS view result with a direct table SELECT using the same conditions to isolate whether the issue is in the view's join logic, filter, or the source data itself. Check SE09 for recent transports to the CDS view or related DCL — any recent change is a prime suspect. Use ST05 SQL trace to capture the actual SQL generated and verify join conditions are correct. Check if DCL authorization filtering is excluding rows the business expects to see — use STAUTHTRACE for this.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Incident: Finance team reported 200 fewer GL accounts appearing in period-end report vs previous month. ST05 trace showed correct SQL. Direct BSEG count matched CDS view count. Checked SE09: three weeks ago, DCL was updated with new profit center restriction. 200 GL accounts were from a profit center the users did not have authorization for — but they previously had it before the DCL change. Resolution: DCL updated to restore access for the finance team's profit centers. Root cause: DCL change was tested with admin user (all authorizations) — not tested with actual finance user roles.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Wrong data incident mein immediately CDS view code fix karna without first understanding if it is a data issue or view logic issue. Data issue aur view logic issue alag cheezein hain. First isolate — direct table SELECT se compare karo — phir decide karo kahan fix hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
* Investigation: Compare CDS view vs direct table

" Step 1: CDS view result count
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM zcds_revenue_report
  WHERE FiscalYear = '2024' AND CompanyCode = '1000'
  INTO @DATA(lv_cds_count).

" Step 2: Direct table count (same conditions)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM acdoca
  WHERE gjahr = '2024' AND rbukrs = '1000'
  INTO @DATA(lv_direct_count).

" Step 3: Find the difference
IF lv_cds_count <> lv_direct_count.
  DATA(lv_diff) = lv_direct_count - lv_cds_count.
  WRITE: / 'Missing rows:', lv_diff.
  WRITE: / 'Likely cause: DCL filter or JOIN condition'.
ENDIF.
" ST05 trace running during step 1 shows exact SQL

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you diagnose a slow CDS view in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Slow CDS view diagnosis steps: 1) ST05 SQL trace — exact SQL dekho, runtime dekho. 2) HANA Expensive Statements mein dekho — CDS view ka SQL appear karta hai agar slow hai. 3) Missing index check karo — full table scan to large table. 4) Input parameters use ho rahe hain ya nahi — agar parameters missing toh full table scan. 5) Association resolve ho raha hai unnecessarily — consumer ne unnecessary $expand kiya? 6) Data volume change hua hai — table badi ho gayi, statistics outdated.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS view performance diagnosis: Start with ST05 SQL trace to capture the exact SQL statement generated by the CDS view and its runtime. Look for full table scans on large tables — the HANA execution plan shows this. Check HANA Expensive Statements monitoring for CDS-generated SQL appearing there. Verify that input parameters are being passed if the view is parameterized — missing parameters cause full table scans. Check if associations are being unnecessarily resolved — $expand on large associations without WHERE conditions pulls excessive data. Check if table statistics are outdated — HANA may use wrong execution plan. Finally, check if recent data volume growth has made existing indexes insufficient.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Performance incident: Purchase order Fiori app slow since Monday — list page 18 seconds (was 2 seconds). ST05 trace: CDS view generating SQL with full table scan on EKKO (8 million rows). Root cause found: last Friday transport changed the WHERE clause in CDS view — removed plant filter that was previously pushed down. Without plant filter, full EKKO scan happens. Emergency fix: restore plant filter from WHERE clause. Transport took 45 minutes for DEV-QA-PRD cycle. Interim workaround: service team added URL filter parameter from Fiori app side. Production stabilized in 2 hours.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Production performance issue ke liye immediately CDS view change karna without diagnosis. Ye zyada break kar sakta hai. Pehle ST05 trace se root cause confirm karo, phir development mein fix banao, quality mein test karo, phir production transport karo. Emergency workaround (like OData filter from Fiori) buys time for proper fix.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:

SELECT * FROM zcds_po_incident_view
  WHERE DocDate >= '20240101'
    AND DocType = 'NB'
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_po)
  UP TO 100 ROWS.

" Step 2: Check if parameterized view is missing params
" Wrong - no parameters passed (full scan):
" SELECT * FROM zcds_fi_docs_param( ) ... ERROR!

" Step 3: Check HANA - paste generated SQL from ST05
" Into HANA Studio SQL console

" Look for: TABLE SCAN on large tables
" Fix: index on filter column or add WHERE pushdown

" Step 4: Verify fix
" Before: TABLE SCAN EKKO 8M rows - 18 sec
" After: INDEX SCAN EKKO by BEDAT - 0.3 sec

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle an OData service failure related to CDS views?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: OData service failure steps: 1) /IWFND/ERROR_LOG — error message dekho. 2) /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE — service active hai ya nahi check karo. 3) HTTP error code dekho — 401 (auth), 404 (service not found), 500 (runtime error), 503 (service unavailable). 4) CDS view itself test karo ADT F8 se — view kaam kar raha hai ya nahi. 5) Service registration refresh karo agar needed. 6) Gateway server aur backend system connectivity check karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: OData service failure diagnosis: Check /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on the gateway server for HTTP error details and ABAP exception messages. Check /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE to verify the service is registered and active — sometimes a transport overwrites service activation. For 500 errors: check SM21 system log on backend for ABAP exceptions during CDS view execution. For 404: service not found — may need re-registration or activation. For authorization-related 403: STAUTHTRACE to verify DCL is passing. For performance-related 503: check CDS view execution time via ST05.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: OData incident Monday morning: 40 Fiori app users reporting 'service not available'. /IWFND/ERROR_LOG: 'Service ZCDS_PO_SRV not found'. /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE: service was in inactive state. Root cause: Friday transport of a related CDS view accidentally reset the service activation flag. Resolution: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE → Add Service → search ZCDS_PO_SRV → add system alias → activate. Service restored in 10 minutes. All 40 users operational before business started. Post-incident: transport procedure updated to include service activation verification step.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: OData error report hone pe directly CDS view code mein changes karna. CDS view thik ho sakta hai, OData service layer issue ho sakti hai. Diagnosis pehle: /IWFND/ERROR_LOG, /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, SM21 — phir target layer mein fix karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" OData incident diagnostic checklist:

" 1. Check error log (run in SAP GUI)
" Transaction: /IWFND/ERROR_LOG
" Filter: today's date, service name ZCDS_*
" → Error message shows root cause

" 2. Check service activation
" Transaction: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE
" Find service: ZCDS_PO_SRV
" Status should be: Active
" → If inactive: Add Service Alias + Activate

" 3. Test CDS view independently
" Eclipse ADT: F8 on ZCDS_PO_INCIDENT_VIEW
" → If F8 fails: CDS view issue
" → If F8 works: OData layer issue

" 4. Test OData endpoint directly
" Transaction: /IWFND/GW_CLIENT
" URI: /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCDS_PO_SRV/PurchaseOrderSet
" → Check HTTP response code and body

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you roll back a CDS view change that caused a production issue?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS view rollback options: 1) Previous transport karo — agar pehle wali version kisi transport mein hai toh SE10/SE09 se reimport karo. 2) ADT mein source history — versioning compare karo, pehle wala code restore karo aur naya transport banao. 3) Agar change minor tha — production mein directly hotfix transport karo (agar policy allow kare). CDS views ka undo button nahi hota directly — transport process se hi manage hota hai. Rollback plan pehle se socho deployment ke time.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS view rollback requires transporting the previous version. Options: First, check SE10 for the previous transport request containing the last known-good version — request its reimport to production via TMS. Second, in Eclipse ADT, the revision history of CDS views is available — locate the previous version, copy the code, create a new transport with the reverted code. Third, for minor issues: make a targeted fix directly in development, test in quality, and emergency transport to production — faster than full rollback if the change is straightforward. Always document the rollback reason and impact in the transport request for audit trail.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Rollback situation: CDS view change transported on Friday — added a new WHERE clause to filter cancelled orders. Monday: business team reports revenue figures 15% lower — cancelled orders had valid revenue postings that should be included. Analysis: WHERE clause was too broad — filtering valid orders too. Rollback decision: revert WHERE clause change via new transport (not true rollback — previous transport not available in reimport queue). New transport with original WHERE clause built in DEV, tested in QA in 2 hours, transported to production. Total downtime for affected report: 4 hours Monday morning.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Production mein directly CDS view change karna via ADT without proper transport — SAP best practice violation. Changes must go through DEV → QAS → PRD transport path. Emergency hotfix is acceptable if done through proper transport even with expedited timeline. Direct production changes bypass quality testing and create audit issues.

" Step 1: Identify last good transport
" SE10: Transport Organizer
" Filter: Object ZCDS_SALES_ORDER_FULL
" Find: last transport BEFORE the problematic one
" Note: Transport Request number, date

" Step 2a: Reimport previous transport (if available)
" STMS → Import Queue → Production
" Add transport request for reimport
" Execute import → CDS view reverted

" Step 2b: Create revert transport (if 2a not possible)
" ADT: Open CDS view
" History: right-click → Compare with → Local History
" Find version before change, copy code
" Paste in current editor, save, activate
" Assign to new emergency transport request
" Transport DEV → QAS (expedited test) → PRD

" F8 in ADT: confirm view behavior restored
" ABAP test: run report with problem data → correct results

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you prevent CDS view-related production incidents?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Prevention best practices: 1) Quality testing rigorous — multiple user roles, boundary data, performance with prod-like data volume. 2) Transport checklist — DCL, SE11 view, dependent views — sab included. 3) HANA performance test in quality — ST05 trace mandatory before transport approval. 4) Monitoring setup — OData service health check, CDS view execution time alerts. 5) Code review — senior developer approval before transport. 6) Production data volume simulation in quality for performance testing.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Prevention strategies for CDS view incidents: First, comprehensive testing in quality — test with production-like data volume since performance issues often only appear with large datasets. Second, mandatory ST05 SQL trace review before any CDS view transport to verify no full table scans on large tables. Third, transport checklist: CDS view, DCL source, generated SE11 view — all must be included; and transport sequence must be correct (interface before consumption views). Fourth, authorization testing with actual end-user roles, not admin roles — DCL restriction issues are discovered only when tested with the right user. Fifth, change freeze periods aligned with business critical periods (month-end, year-end) for CDS views used in financial reporting.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Prevention implementation after a series of incidents: Team established 'CDS Release Checklist': 1) Code review by senior developer in ADT. 2) ST05 trace in quality with 50,000 record test dataset. 3) Authorization test with three different user role profiles. 4) OData functional test in /IWFND/GW_CLIENT. 5) Transport verification — all related objects included. 6) Peer sign-off on checklist before production transport request. Result: zero CDS-related incidents in the next 6 months after implementing checklist vs 4 incidents in previous 6 months.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Quality testing admin user se karna — admin has all authorizations, all data visible. End-user specific issues (DCL, authorization) nahi dikhenge. Always test with user accounts that have the same role profiles as production end-users. Create test users that mirror real user role assignments for pre-transport testing.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" CDS View Pre-Transport Testing Template

REPORT z_cds_pre_transport_test.

" Test 1: Data volume and performance
DATA: lv_start TYPE timestampl,
      lv_end   TYPE timestampl.
GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_start.
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM zcds_po_incident_view
  INTO @DATA(lv_count).
GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_end.
DATA(lv_duration) = cl_abap_tstmp=>subtract(
  tstmp1 = lv_end tstmp2 = lv_start ).
WRITE: / 'Rows:', lv_count, 'Time(s):', lv_duration.
IF lv_duration > 5. " 5 second threshold
  WRITE: / '*** PERFORMANCE WARNING - Check ST05 ***'.
ENDIF.

" Test 2: Association navigation works
SELECT PurchaseOrder, _Items-matnr AS Mat
  FROM zcds_po_incident_view
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items)
  UP TO 5 ROWS.
WRITE: / 'Items found:', lines( lt_items ).

followupAnswerBank.question: What monitoring do you set up for CDS views in production?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS view monitoring options: 1) HANA Expensive Statements — automatically logs slow SQL statements — CDS-generated SQL bhi aata hai. 2) /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE — OData service health monitoring. 3) Custom ABAP monitoring — scheduled program jo CDS view health check kare aur SLG1 mein log kare. 4) Basis team alerting — system performance alerts jo HANA cockpit se configure hote hain. 5) Application log SLG1 — agar consuming programs log karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS view monitoring in production: HANA Expensive Statements view (M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS) automatically captures SQL exceeding configured thresholds — CDS-generated SQL appears here when slow. Configure HANA alert thresholds for statement execution time. For OData-based CDS services, /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE shows service status, and /IWFND/ERROR_LOG captures runtime errors. Custom monitoring: scheduled ABAP program that periodically executes COUNT on critical CDS views, measures runtime, and alerts via SLG1 or email if threshold exceeded. For SAP Solution Manager users: CCMS alerts can be configured for OData service health.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Monitoring setup after incident: Created a simple monitoring report Z_CDS_HEALTH_CHECK running hourly via job. Report executes SELECT COUNT on 10 critical CDS views, measures time, compares against threshold (5 seconds max). If any view exceeds threshold — SLG1 entry with severity 'Error', email to basis team and functional lead. First week: caught one CDS view degrading gradually (statistics outdated) before users noticed. Basis updated HANA statistics, performance restored. Proactive catch vs reactive incident.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Monitoring setup karna sirf after first incident. Production monitoring CDS views ke liye deploy ke saath honi chahiye. Performance baseline establish karo at go-live — normal runtime measure karo — phir alerts configure karo uske against. Without baseline, threshold kya set karo pata nahi hoga.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
* Simple CDS health monitoring program
REPORT z_cds_health_monitor.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_view,
         name      TYPE string,
         threshold TYPE i,  " seconds
       END OF ty_view.

DATA: lt_views TYPE TABLE OF ty_view.
APPEND VALUE #( name = 'ZCDS_PO_INCIDENT_VIEW' threshold = 3 ) TO lt_views.
APPEND VALUE #( name = 'ZI_SALES_ORDER_FULL'   threshold = 5 ) TO lt_views.

LOOP AT lt_views INTO DATA(ls_view).
  DATA(lv_start) = cl_abap_tstmp=>systimestamp( ).
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (ls_view-name)
    INTO @DATA(lv_cnt).
  DATA(lv_secs) = cl_abap_tstmp=>subtract(
    tstmp1 = cl_abap_tstmp=>systimestamp( )
    tstmp2 = lv_start ).
  IF lv_secs > ls_view-threshold.
    " Log to SLG1 with severity E (Error)
    WRITE: / 'ALERT:', ls_view-name, lv_secs, 'sec - threshold exceeded'.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle an incident where a CDS view DCL is blocking all users?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DCL blocking incident: sabse pehle confirm karo ki DCL hi issue hai. STAUTHTRACE se trace karo — kaunsa authorization check fail ho raha hai. Options: 1) DCL temporarily disable karo (@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED change karo) — CAUTION: security risk, sirf extreme emergency. 2) Missing role/permission users ko add karo quickly via PFCG. 3) DCL source mein specific condition relax karo aur emergency transport karo. 4) Authorization object value range expand karo basis team se. DCL issue mein security team aur basis team involve karna zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DCL blocking incident: First confirm with STAUTHTRACE that DCL is the cause — run it as the affected user and check which authorization object check is failing. If DCL is blocking legitimately due to missing roles: coordinate with security team to urgently add missing role to affected users in PFCG and sync via SU10. If DCL itself has incorrect logic from a recent change: evaluate whether temporarily relaxing the DCL (emergency transport with broader condition) or rolling back to previous DCL version is faster. Disabling DCL entirely (@NOT_REQUIRED) is an absolute last resort that requires security team sign-off due to data exposure risk.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: DCL incident: After go-live, entire accounts payable team (50 users) could not see any vendor data in Fiori app. STAUTHTRACE: authorization object M_LFM1_EKO (purchasing org field EKORG) failing for all AP users. DCL required EKORG in user's role — AP team's roles had BUKRS (company code) but not EKORG. Purchasing org authorization was a new DCL requirement not present in old program. Emergency fix: PFCG role for AP team updated to include all purchasing orgs within their company codes. SU10 mass role sync. 50 users operational within 45 minutes. Root cause: DCL go-live testing was done with basis admin user who had all auth objects — AP team roles were never tested.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: DCL incident mein @NOT_REQUIRED lagana without security team approval and documentation. Ye production security audit issue create karta hai. Hamesha security team involve karo DCL-related changes mein — unki sign-off ke bina authorization workarounds mat karo production mein.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you prevent CDS view incidents from recurring?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Recurring incidents prevent karne ke liye: root cause fix karo — sirf symptom mat bandh karo. CDS ke liye: transport checklist banao (DDLS + DCLS + DDLX sab sahi sequence mein). Code review: CDS changes peer review se guzarne chahiye. Testing checklist: DEV ADT preview, QA mein OData test, authorization check bhi. Monitoring: SICF service status, /IWFND/ERROR_LOG regularly check karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Prevent CDS incident recurrence: 1) Root Cause Analysis document. 2) Transport checklist — CDS source, DCL, metadata extension must transport in correct sequence. 3) Pre-transport checklist: activate in DEV, test ADT preview, verify DCL auth. 4) Post-transport verification: check service in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, run smoke test. 5) Monitoring alert for /IWFND/ERROR_LOG. These steps eliminate 90% of recurring CDS incidents.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: After a DCL-missing incident causing data exposure, team implemented: mandatory 5-point CDS transport review checklist, ATC check before transport release, post-transport smoke test calling OData service. Zero CDS-related incidents in 6 months following this process.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Only fixing immediate issue without process improvement. Same CDS transport sequence errors kept happening every sprint until checklist was made mandatory. Process discipline prevents recurrence more than technical fixes alone.
